title: The Sugar (Road Development Cess) Ordinance, 1960 (East Pakistan Ordinance)
title_en: The Sugar (Road Development Cess) Ordinance, 1960 (East Pakistan Ordinance)
description: An Ordinance to levy cess for the development and maintenance of roads in the [Sugar Mill Zone areas in Bangladesh]. WHEREAS it is expedient to levy cess for the development and maintenance of roads in the [Sugar Mill Zone areas in Bangladesh]; NOW, THEREFORE, in pursuance of the Presidential Proclamation of the seventh day of October, 1958, and in exercise of all powers enabling him in that behalf, the Governor o...

provisions.title: 1. Short title, extent and commencement
provisions.content:
1. (1) This Ordinance may be called the [* * *] Sugar (Road Development Cess) Ordinance, 1960.
(2) It extends to the whole of [Bangladesh].
(3) It shall be deemed to have come into force on the 18th day of December, 1959.

provisions.title: 2. Levy of cess
provisions.content:
2. (1) Every person selling sugarcane to the Sugar Mills, shall pay a cess at the rate of [twelve poisha] per maund of sugarcane
sold by him for the development and maintenance of roads in the [Sugar Mill Zone areas]:
Provided that on fraction of a maund of sugarcane supplied at a time, 1/16 of the total amount received as price of sugarcane shall be paid as cess:
Provided further that a Sugar Mill, while making payment of the price of sugarcane to a sugarcane seller, may pay [twelve poisha] less per maund of cane so purchased and pay the cess to the [Government] at the rate of [twelve poisha] per maund on the total maundage of cane purchased.
(2) Every Sugar Mill who use sugarcane from their own farm for the production of sugar, shall also pay the cess at the rate prescribed in sub-section (1) above.

provisions.title: 3. Mode of collection and expenditure of cess
provisions.content: [3. The amount of cess shall be collected by the Sugar Mills on behalf of the Government for each maund of sugarcane sold to them by the sugarcane sellers or used from their own farm, as the case may be, and shall be deposited into the local treasury under the head "F. 5-Deduct-Receipts and Recoveries on Capital Accounts- F.5(4)-Sugar Development Cess from Sugar growers” for the purpose of credit of the collection, and the expenditure for the purpose of maintenance and development of roads in the sugarcane growing areas shall be debitable under the head “F-Sugar Development- F.2(3)-Payment to Sugar Cess Road Development Committee” subordinate to the major Head “85-A-Capital Outlay on Schemes of State Trading- (Misc.) C-Other Miscellaneous Schemes”.

provisions.title: 4. Committee
5. Submission of returns
provisions.content: 4. Each Sugar Mill shall submit a monthly statement by the first week of the following month to the [Secretary, Ministry of Industries] showing the total quantity of sugarcane purchased by them from the sugarcane sellers or used from their own farm, and the total amount of cess realised and deposited by them. The Sugar Mills shall also send along with the statement a copy of the Treasury chalan showing such deposit.

provisions.title: 6. Audit of Accounts
provisions.content: [5. The cess collection accounts of Sugar Mills and the accounts of the Sugar Cess Road Development Committees shall be audited by the Government Commercial Audit and necessary facilities shall be afforded to them by the Sugar Mills and the Sugar Cess Road Development Committees for the purpose.]

provisions.title: 7. Penalty
provisions.content: 6. If the person on whom the cess is levied under section 2, or who is responsible for the collection and payment of the cess under section 3, fails to pay the cess as provided in this Ordinance, he shall be liable to a penalty not exceeding the amount of the cess payable.

provisions.title: 8. Power to make rules
provisions.content: 7. The [Government] may make rules for carrying out the purposes of this Ordinance .
